etymology.description: 大(大) (dà, "big; great") signifies largeness or importance, while 安(安) (ān, "peace; calm") suggests tranquility and safety. Combined, 大安 (dà’ān) conveys a sense of "great peace".
etymology.interpretation: The concept of 大(大) (dà, "big; great") coupled with 安(安) (ān, "peace; calm") suggests an overarching or vast state of tranquility. This can imply societal harmony or personal well-being experienced on a grand scale, hence "great peace". The use of 大(大) (dà, "big; great") emphasizes an enhanced level of 安(安) (ān, "peace; calm"), indicating not just ordinary peace, but a significant, prevailing peace.

examples.chinese: 大安是一个理想的居住地(大安是一個理想的居住地)。
examples.pinyin: Dà’ān shì yī gè lǐxiǎng de jūzhù dì.
examples.english: Da’an is an ideal place to live.

examples.chinese: 他们计划去大安公园野餐(他們計劃去大安公園野餐)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen jìhuà qù Dà’ān gōngyuán yěcān.
examples.english: They plan to have a picnic in Da’an Park.

examples.chinese: 大安给人一种宁静的感觉(大安給人一種寧靜的感覺)。
examples.pinyin: Dà’ān gěi rén yī zhǒng níngjìng de gǎnjué.
examples.english: Da’an gives a sense of tranquility.
